Task: Open a web browser
Example Instruction: Open Safari
Context: I have some free time and would like to browse the internet.
End State: Safari "Home" screen
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (autoplaces in search field)

Task: Navigate to the search field
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m ready to begin browsing. Can you take me to the search field?
Example Instruction: Open Wikipedia
Context: I'm ready to begin browsing the web.
End State: Wikipedia screen
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (auto places in search field)

Task: Move to the previous page
Example Instruction: Go back to the previous page
Context: I missed something on the previous page that i wanted to reread.
End State: Safari "home" screen
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (auto places in search field)/Search something/Click back arrow

Task: Create a tab
Example Instruction: Create a new tab
Context: I'm enjoying browsing the web, but need a new tab to stay organized.
End State: Safar "home" screen (from new tab)
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (auto places in search field)/Search something/Create new tab

Task: Bookmark a website
Example Instruction: Bookmark a website
Context: I want to revisit this website in a little bit.
End State: Wikipedia appears in bookmarks
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (auto places in search field)/Search something/Click share button/Click "Add Bookmark"/Title and click "Add

Task: Search for a product on an online store, with a filter applied
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: Hey, I’m looking to purchase an item on an online sotre. Can you search for a product and apply a filter to help me narrow down the results?
Example Instruction: Go to Amazon and search for "frying pan" and filter the results by "10 to 11" inches
Context: I've been getting into cooking recently, so I've decided to upgrade my frying pan.
End State: Amazon is loaded on a web browser. The search field reads "frying pan" and the "10 to 11" filter check box is checked.
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (autoplaces in search field)/Search "Amazon.com"/Navigate and click the Amazon search field/Search "frying pan"/Select "10-11 inch" filter

Task: Add a product to the shopping cart on an online store
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I found a product I like and am ready to purchase it. Can you place the item in my shopping cart?
Example Instruction: Search for "potting soil" on the Walmart website and add the most popular (first search result) item to my cart
Context: I want to start a garden in my backyard
End State: The Walmart website is loaded and an indicator by the shopping cart logo appearsa, notifying an item is in it (first item found by "potting soil").
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/ Click Safari (auto places in search field)/Search "Target.com"/Navigate and click the Target search field/Search "potting soil"/Add first result to cart

Task: Create a new account
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I've been using a website i really enjoy recently and would like to create an account to make the checkout process easier. Can you create an account?
Example Instruction: Create a Target account
Context: I've been using the Target website recently and would like to make an account for ease of use
End State: Target verification of an account created is present
Resources we need: Pre-created Username and Password

Task: Log in to an online account
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I haven’t used my account in a while, so I need to log in. Can you log in for me with the given username and password?
Example Instruction: Log in to the TArget account
Context: I hthought I would use the Target website more since I created an account, but I haven't. I want to start using it more, but it's been awhile and I'm logged out.
End State: The Target website shows a screen where an account is logged into
Resources we need: Pre-created Username and Password

Task: Delete an online account
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m not using my account on this website anymore. Can you delete it?
Example Instruction: Delete the Target account
Context: I've had this account for the Target website for awhile and I'm just not using it.
Resources we need: Pre-created Username and Password

Task: Open a link from a document
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m reading a paper and am really enjoying it. There are some sources linked at the bottom and I’m curious about them. Could you open one of the links?
Example Instruction: Open the second link in Google Docs
Context: I'm reading a research article about the solar system in Google Docs and am enjoying it. At the bottom there are references in the form of links. i would like to eplore these
End State: The screen of the second link of the research article on the solar system is present
Resources we need: An official research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (copied and pasted in a Google Doc) or a short hand-made research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom

Task: Close a tab
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: Can you close a tab in my browser?
Example Instruction: Close a tab
Context: I'm browsing the web right now, but it's getting a little cluttered
End State: Safari "home" screen form new tab
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (auto places in search field)/Search something/Create new tab/Close tab

Task: Create and open a new file in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: Hi, I got assigned a project and need to create a new file in Google Docs. Can you create a new file in Google Docs for me?
Example Instruction: Create a new Google Doc and name it "Study Plan
Context: I'm making a study plan for an upcoming coding test.
End State: A blank file in Google Docs is created named "Study Plan"is present
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to application list (bottom of screen)/Click Safari (auto places in search field)/Search for "Google Docs"/Click "Sign in" and proceed with log in series (depends on user)/Click "+" to create a new Doc/Click the textbox to rename the file to "Study Plan

Task: Place cursor on a cell in Google Sheets
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm editing a Google Sheet and would like to begin. Can you plac eyour cursor on a cell?
Example Instruction: Put cursor on cell A1
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summer in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I took a break, but am ready to get back into it.
End State: Cell A1 is selected
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Download a file to a new folder
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm working on a file and am ready to save it to a folder to share with my teacher. Can you download the file to a folder?
Example Instruction: Download the Google Doc and name it "Solar System
Context: I'm reading a research article on the solar system in Google docs and am interested in continuing reading it.
End State: The Google Doc appears in the new folder "Solar system
Resources we need: An official research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (copied and pasted in a Google Doc) or a short hand-made research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom

Task: Create a new folder
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I want to organize my files a little better. Can you help me create a new folder?
Example Instruction: Create a folder named "Solar system paper" so I can save it there
Context: I've been reading a research article on the solar system in Google Docs and want to keep reading it later.
End State: A new folder named "Solar system" is created
Steps Breakdown: Click "+" button/Click "Folder"/Type "Solar System"/Click Enter

Task: Increase the font size in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I realized the font in Google Docs is a little too small for most readers. Can you increase the font size?
Example Instruction: Increase font size to 20
Context: I'm reading a research article on the solar system in Google Docs. This is not the original copy, so I can make some edits. The text is a little small in my opinion.
End State: The font on the research article in Google Docs is increased to 20

Task: Change the font color in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m creating a header and want to play around with some different colors. Can you change the font color?
Example Instruction: Change the font color to "Red3

Task: Change the font style in Google Docs

Example Instruction: Change the font style of the headers to "Times New Roman

Task: Double space the document in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m working on an assignment and it requires a double spaced format. Can you double space the document for me?
Example Instruction: Double space format the Google Doc
Context: I'm reading a research article on the solar system in Google Docs. This is not the original copy, so I can make some edits. I like to have more space in between lines for ease of read.

Resources we need: An official research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (copied and pasted in a Google Doc) or a short hand-made research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom

Task: Create a new slide in Google Slides
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m working on a presentation in Google Slides and am ready to expand the slide deck. Can you create a new slide?
Example Instruction: First create a new Google Slide and title it "Introduce Yourself". Add a new "Title and body' slide to the slide deck so I can keep adding information
Context: I just had my first day of school and the teacher asked us to make a Google Slides introducing ourselves.
End State: A new "Title and body" page is added to the Google Slides
Steps Breakdown: click "Slide" at the top/Click "+" to add a new slide ("Title and body" is the auto layout)

Task: Fill up a slide with some content
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I have an assignment in google Slides. Can you add some information to get started?
Example Instruction: Add your name to the "Title" slide and some information about you (hobby, favorite color, favorite food, etc) in the body of the "Title and body" slide? Title this slide "Fun Facts".
Context: I just had my first day of school and the teacher asked us to make a Google Slides introducing ourselves.
End State: Their name is added to the "Title" slide and some personal information is added to the "Title and body" slide. "Fun Facts" is given as the title of the "Title and body" slide.
Steps Breakdown: Click the first "Title" slide on the deck view/Click the main box and type your name/Click the "Title and body" slide on the deck view/Click the main box and type "Fun Facts"/Click the body box and type some fun facts

Task: Change the theme in google Slides
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m making a presentation and want to change the appearance to stand out. Can you change the theme of the slide deck?
Example Instruction: Change the theme to "Plum
Context: I just had my first day of school and the teacher asked us to make a Google Slides introducing ourselves. I want to stand out, so a theme would be nice.
End State: The theme of the Google Slides is now "Plum
Steps Breakdown: Click "Slide" at the top/Click "Change Theme"/Click "Plum

Task: Star a Google Slides file
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m working on many presentations right now, however this one needs to be returned to in a few days. I don’t want to search for it, so can you help me start this file?
Example Instruction: Star the Googl eSlide
Context: I just had my first day of school and the teacher asked us to make a Google Slides introducing ourselves. I'm done working on the presentation for now, but don't want to keep searching for it.
End State: The Google Slides (Introduce yourself" star file toggle is now on
Steps Breakdown: Click the star next to the title of the slideshow

Task: Expand a sheet in Google Sheets (new column or row, above or below)
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m working on a spread sheet, but realized I misaligned the sheet. Can you expand the sheet by adding a new row or column, above or below?
Example Instruction: Add a column to the Google Sheet named "Author" to the right of "Book Title
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summer in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I want to add a new column to the right of "Book Title" named "Author".
End State: A new column is added to the right of "Book Title" named "Author
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Create a chart in Google Sheets
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m looking to visually display my data. Can you create a chart out of my data?
Example Instruction: Create a "Column Chart" (x-axis titled "Book Title", y-axis titled "Days to Finish", chart titled "Days to finish for Each Book").
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summery in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I want to visually represent the "Days to Finish" column.
End State: A "Column Chart" is added with the x-axis titled "Book Title", y-axis titled "Days to Finish", and chart titled "Days to finish for Each Book
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Add a formula in Google Sheets
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m looking to do some summary statisitcs for my data. Can you add a formula in the sheet?
Example Instruction: Average the ratings in "Rating (1-10)" using the "Average" formula. Post the results at the bottom of the column.
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summer in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I want to run some summary statistics.
End State: The average of the ratings for "Rating (1-10)" is reported at the bottom of the column, utilizing the "Average" function
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Rename columns in Google Sheets
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm working on a Google Sheet, however I'm not liking the names of the columns I initially gave. Can you rename them?
Example Instruction: Rename the column "Book Title" to just "Title
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summer in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I'm not liking the names "Book Title" I initially gave a column.
End State: The column titled "Book Title" now reads "Title
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Reorder columns In Google Sheets
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I am working on a spreadsheet in Google Sheets and realized I reversed the column order. Can you reverse them for me?
Example Instruction: Reorder columns so that "Rating (1-10)" comes before "Days to Finish".
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summer in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I want to rearrange the columns ab bit.
End State: The column titled "Rating (1-10)" is now before the column titled "Days to Finish
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Stretch an image in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’ve pasted a picture I’m using for a cover page of an article I’m writing in Google Docs. The image needs to be bigger, so can you stretch the image proportionally?
Example Instruction: Stretch/enlarge the diagram within the Google Doc

End State: The diagram expands evenly in the research article on the solar system in Google Docs

Task: Add a strike-through in Google Docs

Example Instruction: Put a strike-through regarding the title of the article
Context: I'm reading a research article on the solar system in Google Docs. This is not the original copy, so I can make some edits. I don't like the title of the article.

Task: Duplicate a slide in Google Slides
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m working on a slideshow in Google Slides and need to duplicate a slide to edit differently. Can you duplicate the last slide for me?
Example Instruction: Duplicate the "Title and body" slide

Steps Breakdown: Click on the second slide/Click command C to copy/Click command v to paste

Task: Align in Google Docs

Example Instruction: Alternate right and left alignment for each section (starting with left)

End State: The research article in Google Docs is alternating right and left aligned by section

Task: Remove/add highlight in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: Someone edited my document in Google Docs, however they highlighted my document. Can you remove this?
Example Instruction: Highlight the headers of the article in Yellow1

End State: The research article's headers are highlighted in Yellow1 in Google Docs

Task: Change slide orientation in Google Slides
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm working on an introduction Google Slide for my new job. I pasted a photo of myself in one of the slides, however I think it would look better if it was vertically oriented. Can you vertically orient the slide with the photo?
Example Instruction: Add a "Blank" slide on the end of the slide deck
Context: I just had my first day of school and the teacher asked us to make a Google Slides introducing ourselves. I'm adding a photo of myself to my Google Slide presentation. I want ot add a photo on a "Blank" slide.
End State: A "Blank" slide is added and it's rotated vertically in Google Slides

Task: Replace a string of words in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m writing a lesson plan for my class in Google Docs and wrote “test”, however it needs to be called “assignment”. Can you replace all instances with the new word?
Example Instruction: Under the section "Knowledge of the Solar System", can you change the sentence "The solar system has been a topic of study from the beginning of history" to "The solar system is one of the most unique entities in history."?
Context: I'm reading a research article on the solar system in Google Docs. This is not the original copy, so I can make some edits. There's one sentence I particularly don't like.
End State: The sentence "The solar system has been a topic of study from the beggining of history." is ced with "The solar system is one of the most unique entities in history.
Resources we need: An official research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (copied and pasted in a Google Doc) or a short hand-made research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (Title needs to be edited to lowercase)

Task: Input data into Google Sheets
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I have some data stored in a Google Doc and need to put it into a Google Sheet to run some summary statistics. Can you do this?
Example Instruction: Paste the data from Google Docs into the Books Google Sheet
Context: I recently got into reading and am tracking all the books I've read this summer in a Google Sheets. I have columns for "Book Title", "Days to Finish", and "Rating (1-10)". I have some data in Google Docs that follow these columns, but need to be pasted into the main Google Sheet.
End State: The data from Google Docs is pasted into the Google Sheets. The columns with the correct information appear and all data is aligned properly.
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).
Steps Breakdown: Navigate to the file containing the initial data and coy it/Navigate to Google Sheets and paste the data  in/Format the Google Sheet correctly to align with columns and rows properly

Task: Change the first letter of each sentence to a capital in GoogleDocs

Example Instruction: Change the title of the article to all uppercase

End State: The title of the research article is all uppercase

Task: Create a blank page in Google Docs
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m writing a book in Google Docs, however I need a blank page added. Can you do this?
Example Instruction: Add a blank page to the bottom of the article

Task: Convert all .docx files to PDF files
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I have to submit my work for an upcoming conference, but all my work. Is in .docx format and they need to be in PDF format. Can you convert all .docx files to PDF files?
Example Instruction: Convert all .docx files to PDF files

Task: Find the minimum value In Google Sheets

Example Instruction: Place a MIN function directly below the "Rating (1-10)" column

End State: The minimum rating is placed at the bottom of the"Rating (1-10)" utilizing the "MIN" function

Task: Export “notes” on the desktop to the Notes section in Google Slides
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m giving a presentation tomorrow, however my “notes” are located on the desktop. Can you export these into the Notes section in Google Slides?
Example Instruction: Paste the notes saved in the Google Doc to the "Notes" section in the Introduce Yourself Google Slide
Context: I just had my first day of school and the teacher asked us to make a Google Slides introducing ourselves. I have some notes saved on my desktop in a Google Docs of some speaking reminders.

Task: Search for a restaurant on Google Maps and provide the address and phone number
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m looking into some new restaurants to try, but I need to know the address to let my friends know where it is. I also want to know the phone number to contact them about some allergy concerns. Can you find the address and phone number of a restaurant on Google Maps?
Example Instruction: Find the address and phone number for "Olive Garden" on East Eisenhower Parkway

Steps Breakdown: Type "Olive Garden"/Click the correct search result (based on address)/Scroll to view speciffied information (exact address, phone number)

Task: Copy an image and paste it in a Google Doc

Task: Multiply two cells in Google Sheets and adjust the decimal
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm working in a spreadsheet right now and am multiplying cells together. The issue is these cells contain decimals, so can you multiply these cells together, but report the answer as whole numbers?
Example Instruction: Multiply "Hourrly Wage" by "Hours Worked" and report hte total in Total Amount". Adjust the results to be whole numers.
Context: I’m calculating my total wage for the day, so I’m multiplying the hours I worked today by my hourly wage. In Google Sheets, the decimal is way too long as I need whole numbers.
End State: The cells are multiplied correctly and are given as a whole number

Task: Change the background color in Google Slides
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I’m working on a presentation in Google Slides, however I want to stand out. Can you change the background color of each slide to red?
Example Instruction: Change the background of each slide to Blue2

End State: The background of each slide is changed to Blue2
Steps Breakdown: Select all slides with shift down arrow/Click "Slide" at the top of the screen/Click "Change background"/Click a blue color/Click "Done

Task: Search Video on Youtube
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm taking a break right now. Can you search for a video on YouTube?
Example Instruction: Play the video "Baby Shark" by "Cocomelon" on YouTube
Context: I'm working in a day care this summer and the kids love "Baby Shark". Can you search for this on YouTube?

Task: Transfer information across domains (information froma folder into Google Sheets)
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm working on a Google Sheet, however the data I need to input is stored in a folder. Cam you tranfer the data from the folder to the Google Sheet?

I have some books I read during the summer before I started this Google Sheets. However, the data is stored in a folder on the Desktop.
End State: The  data of books in the folder on the Desktop is now correctly reported, following the columns of the book reporting Google sheet
Resources we need: A spreadsheet with three columns ("Book Title", "Days to Finish", "rating (1-10)"). Some books (10) need to be added, following these columns ("Days to Finish" and "Rating (1-10)" can be made up).

Task: Extract multiple authors from articles (Google Docs) and place them in a Google Sheet
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm reading a few research articles right now in Google Docs. I like these authors and want to read more by them. Can you extract the author names and place them in Google Sheets
Example Instruction: Extract the authors from the three Solar System based articles and compile them in a Google Sheet titled "Solar System". Create a header titled "Authors" in cell A1 above the authors' names.
Context: I'm reading a research article on the solar system in Google Docs. This is not the original copy, so I can make some edits. Because I enjoyed this article so much, I found two others that are similar. I want to extract the author names form each article and compile them in a Google Sheet with a header that reads "Authors", so i can remember these names for later.
End State: The authors for the solar system research articles in Google Docs are compiled in Google Sheets with a header that reads "Authors
Resources we need: An official research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (copied and pasted in a Google Doc) or a short hand-made research article on the solar system with reference links at the bottom (2 more articles need to be in this format)

Task: Compile information form Google Maps into a Google Sheet
Natural Language Instruction (Placeholde: I'm looking for some new resturaunts to try. Can you compile their names and information in a Google Sheet
Example Instruction: Search for these places on Google Maps and create three columns in Google Sheets (Name, Street, Phone Number) and compile the information.
Context: I'm compiling a list of a few diffeent resturaunts I want to try in Ann Arbor (Olive Garden-East Eisenhower pParkway, HopCat-Dexter Avenue, Knight's Steakhouse-Maynard Avenue).
End State: In a Google Sheet, three columns are made (Name, Street, Phone Number). The three given resturaunts (Olive Garden-East Eisenhower Parkway, HopCat-Dexter Avenue, knight's Steakhouse-Maynard Street) are searched on Google Maps. The information for these resturaunts are compiled and organized correctly in the created Google Sheets
Steps Breakdown: In Google Sheets, click the corresponding columns to title the columns ("Name", "Street", "Phone Number")/Click Google Maps tab/Click the Google Maps search field/Type "Olive Garden"/Cllick the corresponding search result/Click the Google Sheets tab/Click the columns to fill out the corresponding information/Click Google Maps tab/Click the Google Maps search field/Type "Hopcat"/Click the corresponding result/Click the Google Sheet tab/Click the columns to fill out the corresponding information/Click the Google Maps tab/Click the Google Maps search field/Type "Knight's Steakhouse"/Click the corresponding result/Click Google Sheets tab/Click the columns to fill out the corresponding information
